danger_signals.visual_form: Mobile coffin enemies with religious iconography

Structural instability; collision hazard; time pressure

danger_signals.meaning: Environmental death trap; precision navigation required

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Whip as signature weapon; holy water and religious sub-weapons
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Holy items (rosary); churches and religious architecture represent allied spaces

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark purples, blacks, reds; gothic decay
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Crosses (inverted/removed in international versions, suggesting desecration); coffins; blood imagery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bat variants, skeletal enemies, coffin creatures, possessed statues
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Graveyard; dungeon; torture chamber; treasury; demonic castle architecture; religious iconography corrupted or removed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Religious symbols (crosses) removed or defaced in international version; nude statues; blood-filled pools; dungeon torture implements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies spawn continuously; time limit pressure; boss patterns intensify as Simon progresses deeper

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health gauge empties; Simon sprite may show damage state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Life lost; game over triggered if all lives consumed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Blood pools glow red (or green acid in international versions); spikes protrude; platforms move or fall
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Simon takes damage; knockback occurs; forced crouch animation on high falls

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite vanishes; Magic Crystal drops with visual effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Level progression unlock; health fully restored; next stage becomes accessible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Japanese version: blood dripping logo, cross-filled environments, white text story. International version: logo static, crosses removed, rocks replace crosses, blood becomes green acid, story in green HUD font
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game censorship removes religious/violent imagery in Western release; mechanical gameplay remains identical

environmental_storytelling.example: Japanese version features prominent crosses in graveyard, password screen (Jesus Christ), coffins, and background details. International versions have crosses systematically removed or replaced with rocks/tombstones, signaling regional content policies and recontextualization of sacred space as defiled.
Dungeon stage blood pools and ceiling drips changed from red to green in international versions, recontextualizing fatal hazards as chemical rather than visceral threats, reducing horror tone.
Nude statues in Main Hall clothed in international versions, shifting the visual language from desecration/blasphemy to architectural decay.
Moving blocks, falling platforms, and vertical crushes visually communicate death traps without explicit gore; collision detection disabled during ring-swinging indicates safe mechanical zones.
Each stage environment (Stables, Graveyard, Dungeon, Treasury) escalates gothic horror through visual complexity and hazard density, structuring narrative progression through space.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Forced crouch animation; can be canceled by continuous jumping to avoid lag/delay
8-directional attack capability; can be canceled early; limp whip mode allows blocking and weak damage; whip length upgradeable via Morning Star item
Grapple/swing mechanic engaged; collision with level geometry disabled; vertical boost on release from above ring; can chain height gains by repeated hits
Can pass through moving blocks without crush damage; enables wall-entry via collision exploitation
Active aggression; continuous spawning; some cause frame lag when killed (elaborate death animations); collision detection reduces during high lag
Vanish with visual effect; drop Magic Crystal; stage advancement triggered
Mobile attack units; cross-marked in Japanese version (removed international); aggressive pursuit pattern

notes: Super Castlevania IV uses environmental design, hazard visualization, and item placement to communicate world state. Primary distinction between Japanese and international versions: systematic removal of Christian iconography (crosses, Jesus Christ imagery, blood) in Western release, replaced with censored visuals (green acid, clothed statues, rocks). This represents content policy enforcement rather than mechanical change. Whip mechanics enable safe traversal (ring-swinging disables collision), creating visual/mechanical symbiosis where grapple points signal safe passages. Gothic horror communicated through architecture escalation: Stables→Graveyard→Dungeon→Treasury→Castle interior, each with increased hazard density. Lag exploitation in speedrunning reveals collision detection weakness under load—mechanically significant but not intentional world-state communication.
